div.h1: Log2 Histogram: Bucket Distribution for Syscall Latency

g.text: 4 | 8 μs | 16 μs | 8 μs | Page cache hit
Example: read() Syscall Latency Distribution (Power-Law Shape)
Total: 64 × 8 = 512 bytes (1 cache line = 64 bytes, ~8 lines)

p.strong: Why log2 histograms work for syscalls:

ol.li: Typical distribution: 90% of syscalls complete in <10μs (cached), 9% in 10μs-1ms (disk), 1% >1ms (network)
Linear buckets waste space: 99% of samples would crowd into first 1000 buckets, leaving 999,000 empty
Log2 matches reality: Bucket widths grow with typical latency variance—more resolution where samples cluster
Percentile calculation: Cumulative sum from bucket 0 gives p50, p95, p99 without storing individual samples

div.p:
🟢 Fast path (<8μs) 🟡 Normal I/O (8-64μs)
🟠 Slow path (64μs-1ms) 🔴 Timeout zone (>1ms)
